input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: If the question uses only a given name (here: "Maurice") and the passages mention the Hart Memorial Trophy (or synonyms) but do not include any winner names or the year a Maurice won it, apply this edit.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing behavior of producing a concise first-hop summary: name the award found in the passages and state that the passages do not provide the winner name or year for the referenced Maurice.
delta_p.change_rule: When composing the summary, explicitly surface the missing-identity bridge and give concrete next-hop retrieval directions instead of asking for permission to search. Say that the intended person named 'Maurice' is unspecified and must be identified; then instruct the next step(s): (a) open the biography page for the Maurice the user meant (if user supplies a full name), (b) otherwise search biographies and Hart Memorial Trophy winner lists for entries matching the name 'Maurice' to find any Hart Trophy wins and extract the year(s). If ambiguity remains, ask the user to provide the full name. Do not guess or state any winner years or specific winners.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not ask the user for permission to use external sources; do not invent or assert any winner names or years; do not output a candidate answer or year; avoid naming a specific Maurice not present in the visible inputs.
delta_p.output_contract: The produced `summary` must include: (1) a one-sentence statement of the first-hop evidence identifying the award (Hart Memorial Trophy); (2) a one-sentence statement that the passages do not contain the winner name or year for the referenced 'Maurice'; (3) a concise bridge statement that the identity of 'Maurice' is missing; (4) explicit, actionable next-hop retrieval directions: open the specified Maurice's biography (if given) or search Hart Memorial Trophy winner lists and candidate Maurice biographies to extract the year(s) of any Hart wins; (5) if the name remains ambiguous, a short clarifying request asking the user to provide the full name. The summary must not contain guessed years or definitive winner claims.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The current prompt asked for a summary but left the next retrieval step vague. This minimal edit converts a clarifying question into explicit bridge identification and concrete retrieval instructions so the next agent can fetch the correct biography or winners list without changing other summarization behavior.

delta_p.trigger: Apply when the question names a person by given name only (e.g., 'Maurice') and the current summary identifies the award from the passages but states that no winner name or year is present.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's existing behavior of (a) succinctly identifying the award present in the passages (e.g., the Hart Memorial Trophy) and (b) noting that the provided passages do not contain any winner names or the year a named person won it. Preserve polite language asking for clarification when the named individual is ambiguous.
delta_p.change_rule: Augment the summary with a concise bridge and a compact retrieval plan: (1) explicitly request the full name if the questioner meant a different person, and otherwise treat the given name as the intended search target; (2) instruct the next retrieval hop to open the biographical page for the named person to check for any Hart Trophy wins and their year(s); (3) if ambiguity remains or the biography lacks year details, instruct retrieval of a chronological list of Hart Memorial Trophy winners to confirm any year(s) associated with that given name. Make these steps explicit and actionable, and do not assert any winner or year in the summary.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assume or assert which specific individual is meant; do not state or imply any winner year or other factual answer not present in the given passages; do not include long quoted spans from unseen sources or introduce sample-specific anchors that are not already present in the visible inputs beyond the given name and the award name.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce an updated `summary` that (A) names the award found in the passages and notes the absence of any winner names or year for the named person, (B) includes a short bridge asking the user to confirm the full name if ambiguous, and (C) gives a compact, ordered retrieval plan: first open the named person's biography to look for Hart Trophy wins and years; if unclear, open a chronological Hart Memorial Trophy winners list to confirm the year(s). The summary must not claim any winner or year, must be concise (a few sentences), and must preserve the original summary tone.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The change minimally strengthens the summary to convert a statement of missing information into a clear, actionable retrieval direction and disambiguation request so the next agent knows exactly which pages to fetch without asserting any new facts.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What year did Maurice win the award given to the  "player judged most valuable to his team" in the NHL?
passages: Arena Football League Most Valuable Player Award | The Arena Football League Most Valuable Player Award (AFL MVP) is an award given by the Arena Football League (AFL) to the arena football player who is considered most valuable to his team in the AFL.  From 1996 to 2010, the League did not award an MVP.

Hart Memorial Trophy | The Hart Memorial Trophy, originally known as the Hart Trophy, is awarded annually to the "player judged most valuable to his team" in the National Hockey League (NHL).  The original trophy was donated to the league in 1923 by David Hart, the father of Cecil Hart, the longtime head coach of the Montreal Canadiens.  The Hart Trophy has been awarded 92 times to 56 different players since its beginnings in 1924.  Each year, members of the Professional Hockey Writers' Association vote to determine the player who was the most valuable to his team during the regular season.

ABA Playoffs Most Valuable Player Award | The ABA Playoffs Most Valuable Player Award was an annual American Basketball Association (ABA) given in the ABA Playoffs.  The award was first awarded in the 1968 ABA Playoffs, and was retired as part of the ABA–NBA merger.  In sports, the player judged to be the most important to the team is the most valuable player (MVP).

Conn Smythe Trophy | The Conn Smythe Trophy (French: "Trophée Conn Smythe" ) is awarded annually to the player judged most valuable to his team (MVP) during the National Hockey League's (NHL) Stanley Cup playoffs.  It is named after Conn Smythe, the longtime owner, general manager, and head coach of the Toronto Maple Leafs.  The Conn Smythe Trophy has been awarded 48 times to 42 players since the 1964–65 NHL season.  Each year, at the conclusion of the final game of the Stanley Cup Final, members of the Professional Hockey Writers' Association vote to elect the player deserving of the trophy.  The trophy is handed out prior to the presentation of the Stanley Cup by the NHL Commissioner and only the winner is announced, in contrast to most of the other NHL awards which name three finalists and are presented at a ceremony.

KBO League Most Valuable Player Award | The KBO League Most Valuable Player Award is given to the player judged the most valuable player in the Korea Baseball Organization (KBO) League.  The most recent winner is Dustin Nippert of the Doosan Bears.

The Sporting News Most Valuable Player Award | "The Sporting News" established "The Sporting News" Most Valuable Player Award in 1929.  The award was given annually to the player judged by "TSN" baseball experts as being the most valuable in each league.  The awards were discontinued in 1946.

ECHL awards | The ECHL presents numerous annual awards to recognize its teams, players, front office staff, and media contributors.  The first awards were given out in recognition of the achievements of the league's teams and players in the league's inaugural season of 1989 and included the Jack Riley Cup and Brabham Cup awarded to the league's teams and the Most Valuable Player, Playoffs Most Valuable Player, Rookie of the Year, Defenseman of the Year, Coach of the Year, and Leading Scorer awards to the league's players.  The league has added many other awards since the league's inception including, the Goaltender of the Year award in 1994, the Sportsmanship Award in 1997, separate titles for each of its conference playoff champions in 1998, and the Plus Performer Award in 2000.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: The passages identify the award as the Hart Memorial Trophy but do not state any winners or the year Maurice won it. I cannot determine the year from the provided passages. Please specify which Maurice you mean or allow me to use external sources to look up the winner year.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.
